title: Adversarial train-test weighting
summary: The data is split by rows but likely contains race/year distribution drift.
rationale: The data is split by rows but likely contains race/year distribution drift. Kaggle tabular practice often uses adversarial validation to reveal train-test mismatch; current CV is random stratified and may reward features that do not transfer across races or seasons.
implementation_hint: Train a lightweight classifier to distinguish train rows from test rows using only non-target features. Use its predicted test-likeness as sample weights for the final model, or build validation folds from the most test-like training rows. Compare against the current random split CV and public score.
expected_effect: Could improve Kaggle public alignment even if local random CV changes little or drops slightly.
risk: If the public test is not representative of the private/test distribution, overweighting test-like rows can reduce generalization.
